currentUse: Unknown

currentUse: Demolished

currentUse: Unknown
situation: Bishop closed the parish in 1947 because he disliked ethnic parishes. Building status unknown.

currentUse: Unknown

currentUse: Apartments; Lithuanian facade inscriptions preserved as a landmark requirement

currentUse: Unknown

currentUse: Demolished; only a cornerstone survives

notes: Unnamed Lithuanian church; abandoned by 2017, razed by 2023; only a cornerstone survives.

currentUse: Private residence; Lithuanian signs removed

notes: Closed; converted to an ordinary residential house; Lithuanian signs removed. Boston suburb, kept separate from Boston proper.

currentUse: Unknown; post-closure community gathered 3x/year
situation: Diocese closed the parish July 1 2008 despite it being solvent, debt-free, with a paid-off school and ~$1M pre-closure renovation. Rescue committee, canon lawyer, and Vatican appeal all failed. Post-closure the community gathered 3x/year.
notes: Closed July 1 2008, merged into Šv. Jono. Solvent, debt-free, with paid-off school; financial health did not save it. Rescue committee, canon lawyer, Vatican appeal all failed. A 2010-03-09 Draugas article (corrected 2010-03-24) records this parish's ~$1M pre-closure renovation and Vytautas Ignas stained glass; post-closure the community gathered 3x/year. The former "Wooster OH" row was this same parish, misspelled — removed 2026-05-23.

currentUse: Non-Lithuanian Catholic use; interior heritage substantially preserved
situation: Transitioned to non-Lithuanian Catholic use by 2017. Interior heritage substantially preserved but Lithuanian identity lost.
notes: Transitioned to non-Lithuanian Catholic use by 2017; interior heritage substantially preserved. Distinct from Worcester Šv. Kazimiero.

currentUse: Demolished
situation: The strongest case in the record: Our Lady of Vilnius, SoHo. Cardinal Egan closed it in 2007 with no debt. NY Court of Appeals ruled against parishioners in 2011. Diocese sold the building, which was demolished. The central ownership test case.
notes: Our Lady of Vilnius, SoHo. The central ownership test case. Closed 2007 by Cardinal Egan; NY Court of Appeals ruled against parishioners 2011; building sold and demolished. Demolition-year conflict (2015 vs 2020) and sale-figure conflict flagged. No debt at closure.

currentUse: Parish formally exists with monthly Lithuanian Mass in rented space; community sold its own buildings
situation: The diocese did not close this parish. The community itself sold its own buildings during decline and moved to rented space. Parish formally still exists with monthly Lithuanian Mass; demographic attrition, not diocesan action.
notes: Diocese did NOT close the parish; the community sold its own buildings during decline and moved to rented space. Parish formally still exists with monthly Lithuanian Mass; demographic attrition, not diocesan action.

currentUse: Demolished; even a photograph could not be found online
situation: Demolished ~2008. So thoroughly erased that even a photograph could not be found online.
notes: Demolished ~2008; even a photograph could not be found online.

currentUse: Non-Roman Anglican cathedral; Lithuanian heritage markers preserved
situation: Diocese closed the parish and sold the building to a non-Roman Anglican body, which uses it as a cathedral. Lithuanian heritage markers preserved.
notes: Closed; building sold by the diocese to a non-Roman Anglican body used as a cathedral. Lithuanian heritage markers preserved.

currentUse: Building intact as of 2019; unused
situation: Diocese of Albany closed the parish ~May 2009. Building intact as of 2019. Demographic collapse left no community to resist.
notes: Closed ~May 2009 (Diocese of Albany); building intact as of 2019. Demographic collapse, no community to resist.

currentUse: Syrian/Assyrian Catholic cathedral; interior retained Lithuanian details
situation: Diocese closed the parish and sold the building; it became a Syrian/Assyrian Catholic cathedral. Interior retained Lithuanian details.
notes: Sold; became a Syrian/Assyrian Catholic cathedral. Interior retained Lithuanian details.

currentUse: Parish-hall basement now in Portuguese/Brazilian use; original church demolished after 1981 fire

notes: Unnamed Lithuanian church; original building demolished after a 1981 fire; diocese refused repairs; congregation moved to the parish-hall basement; now Portuguese/Brazilian use.

currentUse: Grace Gospel Church (Protestant)
situation: Diocese closed the 102-year-old parish March 2 2014; church and rectory sold to Grace Gospel Church (Protestant).
notes: Closed March 2 2014; church and rectory sold to Grace Gospel Church (Protestant). 102-year-old parish; first Lithuanian church 1912, current building 1961/62.

currentUse: Spanish-speaking Pentecostal church
situation: Survived a review then was closed quietly ~end of 2021. Diocese sold the building to a Spanish-speaking Pentecostal church. The Vanagas commemorative plaque was removed and not returned.
notes: Closed quietly ~end of 2021, sold to a Spanish-speaking Pentecostal church. Survived review then closed. The Vanagas commemorative plaque was removed and not returned.

currentUse: Condominiums (apartments as of 2023)
situation: Diocese closed the parish ~2001; building sold ~2007 and converted to condos. Still apartments in 2023.
notes: Closed ~2001; building sold ~2007 and converted to condos. Still apartments in 2023.

currentUse: Sold to non-Catholics; Gediminas-pillars monument remains in churchyard
situation: Diocese closed the parish and sold it ~2015 to non-Catholics. The Gediminas-pillars monument remains in the churchyard.

currentUse: Unknown
situation: First Lithuanian RC parish in New England (founded 1893/94). Survived the 2002 St. Patrick merger; a genuine grassroots revival did not save it. Diocese closed it Sept 30 2023 under the Hartford 2017 plan.
notes: Closed Sept 30 2023 under the Hartford 2017 plan. First Lithuanian RC parish in New England (founded 1893/94). Survived the 2002 St. Patrick merger; a genuine grassroots revival did not protect it.

currentUse: Apartments; commemorative plaques installed
situation: Diocese closed the parish; building converted to apartments with commemorative plaques. Community noted that Greek Orthodox parishes could not be closed because they are a separate religion.
notes: Closed; converted to apartments with commemorative plaques. Community noted Greek Orthodox parishes could not be closed because they are a separate religion.

currentUse: Unknown
situation: Diocese closed the parish June 23 2013 under Together in Faith, merged into Dievo Apvaizdos Southfield. Survived a 1989 closure scare but resistance only delayed the outcome. Only 34 registered parishioners at closure.
notes: Closed June 23 2013 under Together in Faith, merged into Dievo Apvaizdos Southfield. Survived a 1989 closure scare; resistance delayed but could not prevent closure. Only 34 registered parishioners at closure.

currentUse: Unknown; contents shipped to Lithuania
situation: Closed 1995 when the pastor retired; contents shipped to Lithuania. No recorded resistance.
notes: Closed 1995 when the pastor retired; contents shipped to Lithuania. Distinct from Detroit Šv. Antano and Detroit/Southfield Dievo Apvaizdos.

currentUse: Spanish-speaking Catholic congregation
situation: Survived an earlier ~2008 closure danger; ~$60K community savings deemed insufficient. Diocese closed/merged the parish in 2014 into Sts. Peter and Paul; building sold to Spanish-speakers.
notes: Closed/merged 2014 into Sts. Peter and Paul; sold to Spanish-speakers. Šv. Antano draugija founded 1900/01, church consecrated 1907. Survived an earlier ~2008 closure danger; ~$60K community savings deemed insufficient.

currentUse: Derelict; sold by diocese to private buyer

notes: Closed Oct 18 2009, merged into the new consolidated Šv. Kazimiero; old building sold by diocese, now derelict. Buyer-identity conflict flagged.

currentUse: Worship site of the consolidated Sv. Kazimiero Lithuanian parish
situation: Closed Oct 18 2009 and merged with Sv. Jurgio into the new Sv. Kazimiero parish. The former DMNP building became the worship site of the consolidated parish.
notes: Closed Oct 18 2009, merged with Šv. Jurgio into the new Šv. Kazimiero, which worships in the former DMNP building. Distinct from Detroit DMNP.

currentUse: Demolished
situation: The strongest thesis case: solvent parish with ~$1M treasury, a credible $360K repair estimate, landmark status, and a treaty — the diocese demolished it anyway in 2009/2010 and took the ~$1M savings. Coal region.
notes: Closed 2006, demolished 2009/2010. Founding-year MAJOR CONFLICT: 1872, 1874, 1886, 1891 all cited; 1891 is the breakaway Šv. Jurgio parish, building 1894. The strongest thesis case: ~$1M treasury, credible $360K repair estimate, landmark status and a treaty, yet still demolished. Diocese took the ~$1M savings. Coal region.

currentUse: Sold to a private buyer
situation: Diocese closed the parish April 6 2008, merged into a new parish at St. John the Evangelist; later sold to a private buyer. Coal region.
notes: Closed April 6 2008, merged into a new parish at St. John the Evangelist; later sold to a private buyer. Coal region.

currentUse: Demolished; site now a flat field
situation: Diocese demolished one of the two largest, most beautiful Lithuanian churches in the region in 2015. Site is now a flat field. Coal region.
notes: Demolished 2015; site now a flat field. One of the two largest, most beautiful churches in the region. Coal region.

currentUse: Demolished
situation: Diocese demolished the other of the two largest regional Lithuanian churches. Coal region.
notes: Demolished; the other of the two largest regional churches. Coal region.

currentUse: Demolished; only the cornerstone survives
situation: Pittsburgh area. Built 1903; demolished pre-2017 after repeated arson. Only the cornerstone survives.
notes: Pittsburgh area. Built 1903; demolished pre-2017 after repeated arson; only the cornerstone survives. Not anthracite coal region.

currentUse: 12 luxury condominiums

currentUse: Given to convert-priests
situation: The original Lithuanian RC church that Scranton Lithuanians built, then lost to the bishop through the courts — the precipitating event of the LNCC schism. Closed ~2009 in the Allentown/Scranton sweep; building given to convert-priests. Coal region.
notes: RC. Closed ~2009 in the Allentown/Scranton sweep; building given to convert-priests. The original Lithuanian RC church Scranton Lithuanians built then lost to the bishop through the courts; precipitating event of the LNCC schism. Coal region.

notes: RC. Closed as a Lithuanian parish; now a Latin Mass community (Priestly Fraternity of St. Peter); ethnically transferred, not formally dissolved. Coal region.

currentUse: Sold to a private individual; not a community buyout
situation: Diocese closed the parish ~2009 in the Allentown wave; building sold to a private individual (R. Demyanovich, ~$24,000) to prevent warehouse use. Not a community buyout. Coal region.

currentUse: Demolished March 2024; a 1915 time capsule with a Lithuanian letter found in foundations

notes: Founded 1901 (rebuilt 1915); closed ~2001-2002 for mold/moisture; demolished March 2024. A 1915 time capsule with a Lithuanian letter was found in the foundations. Coal region.

currentUse: Part of Divine Redeemer Parish since 1995; school building tax-sold 2022
situation: Founded 1892; diocese closed it 1992; became part of Divine Redeemer Parish 1995. Home of the founding of the Sisters of St. Casimir (1907). School building tax-sold 2022. Coal region.
notes: Founded 1892; closed 1992, became part of Divine Redeemer Parish 1995. Home of the founding of the Sisters of St. Casimir (1907). School building tax-sold 2022. Coal region.

currentUse: Building sold; Jonynas stained glass rescued post-closure by the Saknys project

notes: Closed 2008 under the Allentown plan; building sold; Jonynas stained glass rescued post-closure by the Šaknys project. Coal region.

currentUse: Unknown; final Mass held Jan 5 2026

notes: Lithuanian-immigrant-built 1907; closed as a parish 2015 (merged into St. Charles Borromeo); officially closed as a worship site, final Mass Jan 5 2026. Coal region.

currentUse: Seventh-day Adventist church

notes: Closed 2017 (the only Lithuanian church in Rhode Island); building sold to Seventh-day Adventists. Community declined a diocesan offer to bring in Latin American immigrants.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish with Lithuanian-language Sunday Mass; the only one in Pennsylvania

notes: Survived inside the diocese, confirmed Jan 2026. The only PA Lithuanian parish with a Lithuanian-language Sunday Mass. The separately standing RECTORY was sold ~2023-24 for $1.2M to fund operations; church deed unchanged and still diocese-owned.

currentUse: Open worship site of the merged Philadelphia parish; Lithuanian-Mass continuity conflicted in sources
situation: Survived earlier reviews then was suppressed to worship-site status July 2011. Survives as an open worship site of the merged Philadelphia parish. Lithuanian-Mass continuity after 2011 is conflicted in the sources.
notes: Suppressed to worship-site status July 2011; survives as an open worship site of the merged Philadelphia parish. Survived earlier reviews then was suppressed. Lithuanian-Mass continuity after 2011 is conflicted in the sources.

currentUse: Unknown; merged into an American parish
situation: Survived multiple Philadelphia reviews then was merged into an American parish ~2015-2016, losing its Lithuanian identity.
notes: Merged into an American parish ~2015-2016, losing its Lithuanian identity. Survived multiple Philadelphia reviews then was merged out. Documented only in the Philadelphia Archdiocese plan table and Key Findings (e); no full case file in the document.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; the only Lithuanian church in New England
situation: Survived inside the diocese after a ~20-year campaign; 2021-22 merger deferred. Refused merger consent Feb 2022. The community ceded the deed to the archdiocese in 1904 in exchange for a priest — the only Lithuanian church in New England.
notes: Survived inside the diocese; ~20-year campaign; 2021-22 merger deferred. Community ceded the deed to the archdiocese in 1904 in exchange for a priest. The only Lithuanian church in New England. Refused merger consent Feb 2022.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; on the National Register of Historic Places
situation: Survived inside the diocese; debt-free; on the National Register of Historic Places — the only such Lithuanian church in the corpus. Survived via demonstrated vitality and congregational diversification.
notes: Survived inside the diocese; debt-free; on the National Register of Historic Places (the only such Lithuanian church in the corpus). Parish dedicated 1915; survived via demonstrated vitality and congregational diversification.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish (consolidated from Sv. Jurgio and DMNP)
situation: Consolidated parish formed Oct 25 2009 from the merger of Sv. Jurgio (1895) and DMNP (1929). Survived inside the diocese; a 2014 financial crisis was averted via a diocesan-approved school lease.
notes: Consolidated parish constituted Oct 25 2009 from the merger of Šv. Jurgio Cleveland (1895) and DMNP Cleveland (1929). Survived inside the diocese; a 2014 financial crisis averted via a diocesan-approved school lease.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish (Nativity BVM); sole surviving Lithuanian RC parish in Chicago
situation: Marquette Park. Survived inside the diocese; by 2020 the sole surviving Lithuanian RC parish in Chicago. Chronic deficit covered by the archdiocese as a high-interest loan; building valued ~$20M and not parish-owned.
notes: Marquette Park (Nativity BVM). Survived inside the diocese; by 2020 the sole surviving Lithuanian RC parish in Chicago. Chronic deficit covered by the archdiocese as a high-interest loan; building valued ~$20M and not parish-owned.

currentUse: Active multi-ethnic parish; Lithuanian RC presence and separate Lithuanian Mass continue
situation: Brighton Park (Immaculate Conception). Merged 2019 with Five Holy Martyrs into a multi-ethnic entity, but a Lithuanian RC presence and a separate Lithuanian Mass continue. Both buildings open.
notes: Brighton Park (Immaculate Conception). Merged 2019 with Five Holy Martyrs into a multi-ethnic entity; a Lithuanian RC presence and a separate Lithuanian Mass continue; both buildings open. Filed as standing/surviving.

currentUse: Active parish; formal Lithuanian-parish status transferred to Mexican community 2011, but Lithuanian Masses continued
situation: Survived inside the diocese; small but active. Formal Lithuanian-parish status transferred to the Mexican community in 2011, but Lithuanian Masses continued through at least Dec 2019.
notes: Survived inside the diocese; small but active. Formal Lithuanian-parish status transferred to the Mexican community 2011, but Lithuanian Masses continued through at least Dec 2019.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; sole surviving Lithuanian RC parish in metro Detroit
situation: Survived inside the diocese; the sole surviving Lithuanian RC parish in metro Detroit. Enrolled in Families of Parishes 2025.
notes: RC. Detroit/Southfield. Survived inside the diocese; the sole surviving Lithuanian RC parish in metro Detroit; enrolled in Families of Parishes 2025. Founded 1908 as Šv. Jurgio. Distinct from Scranton LNCC and Chicago 18th-St. Dievo Apvaizdos.

currentUse: Lithuanian mission inside Pasaulio Lietuviu Centras (community-owned venue)
situation: RC ethnic mission established 1990; operates inside the community-owned Pasaulio Lietuviu Centras. Survived an RMC grouping. The venue is community-owned but the mission's canonical function remains diocesan.
notes: RC ethnic mission established 1990; operates inside the community-owned Pasaulio Lietuvių Centras. Survived an RMC grouping. The venue is community-owned but the mission's canonical function remains diocesan.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; major renovation completed 2019; hosts displaced Maspeth Lithuanian-language Masses since Sept 2025
situation: Williamsburg (Annunciation BVM). Survived inside the Diocese of Brooklyn; major renovation completed 2019. Since Sept 2025, hosts the displaced Maspeth Lithuanian-language Masses.
notes: Williamsburg (Annunciation BVM). Diocese of Brooklyn. Survived inside the diocese; major renovation completed 2019; from Sept 2025 hosts the displaced Maspeth Lithuanian-language Masses.

currentUse: Limited-basis opening; closure reversed Sept 12 2025; status unresolved
situation: Queens. Diocese of Brooklyn issued a closure letter Feb 16 2025 but reversed it Sept 12 2025 to limited-basis opening. Status genuinely unresolved as of March 2026. Not closed, no community buyout.
notes: Queens. Diocese of Brooklyn. NOT closed: a closure letter was issued Feb 16 2025 but reversed Sept 12 2025 to limited-basis opening. Survived review then targeted. Status genuinely unresolved as of March 2026; no community buyout.

currentUse: Under financial/maintenance stress; rectory sold, electrical fire; status uncertain

notes: Church built 1895. Under financial/maintenance stress by 2022 (rectory sold, electrical fire); status uncertain, unresolved. One of the last Lithuanian churches in New Jersey.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; daily Masses; one of the strongest Lithuanian bastions in New Jersey
situation: Officially Lithuanian with a Lithuanian Church sign; daily Masses. By 2022 one of the strongest Lithuanian bastions in New Jersey. Survived.
notes: Officially Lithuanian (a Lithuanian Church sign hangs); daily Masses; by 2022 one of the strongest Lithuanian bastions in New Jersey. Survived.

currentUse: Active parish; now majority Spanish-speaking but Lithuanian Masses still held; interior remains very Lithuanian
situation: Survived/transitioned inside the Diocese of Bridgeport. Now majority Spanish-speaking but Lithuanian Masses still held. Interior remains very Lithuanian. Active 2023-24.
notes: Diocese of Bridgeport. Now majority Spanish-speaking but Lithuanian Masses still held; interior remains very Lithuanian. Survived/transitioned, active 2023-24.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; surrounding Lithuanian neighborhood demolished by urban renewal, but church stands
situation: Survived inside the Diocese of Hartford. The surrounding Lithuanian neighborhood was demolished by urban renewal, but the church building stands and was active as of late 2023.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; the one Lithuanian church remaining under Catholic ownership in the 10-church northern PA cluster

notes: The one Lithuanian church remaining under Catholic ownership in the entire 10-church northern PA cluster. Survived. Coal region.

notes: Annunciation BVM. Lithuanian-community-built 1914; still open; hosted the 103rd Lietuvių Dienos festival 2017; adjacent Lithuanian museum since 1982. Survived. Coal region.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish with cultural center

notes: Third building 1951, community-funded but archdiocese-titled. Active cultural center; survived inside the Archdiocese of Los Angeles, confirmed 2020.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; best-preserved living first-wave Lithuanian neighborhood in America
situation: Founded ~1904. Survived; by 2024 described as the best-preserved living first-wave Lithuanian neighborhood in America.
notes: Founded ~1904. Survived; by 2024 described as the best-preserved living first-wave Lithuanian neighborhood in America.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; landmark-designated; still officially Lithuanian after 126+ years
situation: Founded 1895. Survived against extreme odds in the Diocese of Belleville — 11 of 13 East St. Louis Catholic churches closed. Landmark-designated; still officially Lithuanian after 126+ years.
notes: Founded 1895. Diocese of Belleville. Survived against extreme odds (11 of 13 East St. Louis Catholic churches closed); landmark-designated; still officially Lithuanian after 126+ years.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish; temporarily storing Frackville/Shenandoah-area Lithuanian Museum artifacts
situation: Distinct from the closed Sv. Juozapo Waterbury. Survived; still functioning 2022. Temporarily storing the Frackville/Shenandoah-area Lithuanian Museum's artifacts.
notes: Distinct from the closed Šv. Juozapo Waterbury. Survived; still functioning 2022; temporarily storing the Frackville/Shenandoah-area Lithuanian Museum's artifacts.

currentUse: Building used by an American parish; one token Sunday Lithuanian Mass with no Lithuanian priest

notes: Transitioned / de facto closed as a Lithuanian parish; building used by an American parish; one token Sunday Lithuanian Mass with no Lithuanian priest.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian National Catholic parish; community-owned, independent of Rome
situation: LNCC, community-owned, independent of Rome. The last surviving officially Lithuanian parish in the entire NE PA coal region — survives precisely because no bishop can close or sell it. Building 1915. Coal region.
notes: Providence of God. LITHUANIAN NATIONAL CATHOLIC, lay-governed, independent of Rome; community-owned. The last surviving officially Lithuanian parish in the entire NE PA coal region; survives precisely because no bishop can close or sell it. Building 1915. Coal region. Distinct from Detroit/Southfield RC and Chicago 18th-St. RC Dievo Apvaizdos.

currentUse: Sold by the community; proceeds went to the LNCC cemetery fund
situation: LNCC, community-owned. Closed and sold in 2002 by the community itself at parishioners' request; proceeds went to the community's own LNCC cemetery fund. Contrast with the RC parish in the same city closed the same year, where the archdiocese appropriated all property.
notes: LITHUANIAN NATIONAL CATHOLIC, community-owned. Also called Šv. Marijos Tautinė Katalikų Parapija. Closed and sold 2002 BY THE COMMUNITY at parishioners' request; proceeds went to the community's own LNCC cemetery fund. Contrast with RC Šv. Pranciškaus Lawrence same year.

currentUse: Demolished; parish cemetery at Bensalem PA survives
situation: LNCC, community-governed, never diocesan. Broke from RC in the 1916 schism; community sold the building in 1972 with ~30-40 parishioners at closure; building later demolished. Parish cemetery at Bensalem PA survives.
notes: LITHUANIAN NATIONAL CATHOLIC, community-governed, never diocesan. Broke from RC in the 1916 schism; building sold 1972 and demolished; ~30-40 parishioners at closure. Parish cemetery at Bensalem PA survives.

currentUse: Demolished; bell and cornerstone made into a 2004 memorial; cemetery maintained
situation: LNCC, community-governed. Closed ~1969 and demolished by community decision. Bell and cornerstone made into a 2004 memorial; cemetery maintained. Independence removed diocesan risk but not demographic risk.
notes: LITHUANIAN NATIONAL CATHOLIC, community-governed. Closed ~1969 and demolished; bell and cornerstone made into a 2004 memorial; cemetery maintained. Independence removed diocesan risk but not demographic risk.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian Evangelical Lutheran parish; congregationally governed, community-owned; thriving and growing
situation: Lithuanian Evangelical Lutheran, congregationally governed, self-owned (bought its Marquette Park building in 1957). Voted to sell and relocate twice (Western Springs, then Darien Dec 2019); thriving and growing. No bishop can close or sell it.
notes: Lithuanian Evangelical Lutheran, congregationally governed, community-owned (bought its Marquette Park building in 1957). Voted to sell and relocate twice (Western Springs, then Darien Dec 2019); thriving and growing. Self-owned comparator.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian Lutheran congregation; independent, self-governing, founded by a knygnesis from Taurage
situation: Independent self-governing Lithuanian Lutheran congregation, founded by a knygnesis from Taurage. Survived; small, aging, still active as of 2019. Independent of any diocese — no bishop has authority to close it.
notes: Independent self-governing Lithuanian Lutheran congregation, founded by a knygnešys from Tauragė. Survived; small, aging, still active as of 2019. Independent of any diocese.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish under Quebec civil law parish-ownership
situation: Canadian comparator. Under Quebec civil law the parish (not the diocese) is the juridical owner; the diocese cannot act unilaterally. Survived; aging. The ownership-law contrast with U.S. diocese-owned parishes is the point.
notes: CANADA - out-of-scope comparator, NOT counted in the U.S. total of 83. Quebec QC. Under Quebec civil law the parish (not the diocese) is the juridical owner; the diocese cannot act unilaterally. Survived; aging. Natural-experiment comparator.

currentUse: Active Lithuanian parish under Quebec civil law parish-ownership
situation: Canadian comparator. Quebec parish-ownership law means the diocese cannot act unilaterally. Survived; aging. Natural-experiment comparator against U.S. diocese-owned parishes.
notes: CANADA - out-of-scope comparator, NOT counted in the U.S. total of 83. Quebec QC. Quebec parish-ownership law; diocese cannot act unilaterally. Survived; aging. Natural-experiment comparator.

currentUse: Unknown
situation: Canadian comparator. Closed October 2020 after 61 years; the community explicitly framed the closure as self-determination, contrasting it with forced U.S. diocesan closures.
notes: CANADA - out-of-scope comparator, NOT counted in the U.S. total of 83. Ontario ON. Closed October 2020 after 61 years; the community explicitly framed the closure as self-determination, contrasting it with forced U.S. diocesan closures.
